record.field: Mailpit is a fast, single-binary email and SMTP testing tool that captures outgoing mail from your applications and presents it in a modern web interface.

Mailpit is a fast, single-binary email and SMTP testing tool that captures outgoing mail from your applications and presents it in a modern web interface.

It runs an SMTP server that intercepts every message your app sends during development or testing, storing nothing on real inboxes. A built-in web UI lets you inspect captured emails, while a REST API enables automated integration tests. Written in Go with a Vue frontend, it is a drop-in, actively maintained successor to MailHog with the same default ports and a compatible API.

Key features
  • SMTP server with optional STARTTLS/SSL-TLS and authentication that captures all outgoing mail
  • Modern web UI with full-text search, HTML/text/raw views, headers, attachments and real-time WebSocket updates
  • REST API for automated integration testing, plus an optional POP3 server
  • HTML compatibility check, link check and SpamAssassin-based spam scoring
  • Message tagging, plus SMTP relaying/forwarding and a Chaos mode to simulate SMTP failures
  • Single static binary or multi-arch Docker image, low memory footprint, SQLite or PostgreSQL storage with automatic pruning
Best for

Developers and QA teams that need to capture, inspect and test application email during development, CI pipelines and integration testing without sending real messages. An ideal drop-in replacement for MailHog.

services: mailpit: image: axllent/mailpit:v1.22.3 restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8025' volumes: - ./mailpit_data:/data environment: - MP_SMTP_AUTH_ALLOW_INSECURE=true - MP_MAX_MESSAGES=5000 - MP_DATABASE=/data/mailpit.db - MP_UI_AUTH=${MP_UI_AUTH} - MP_SMTP_AUTH=${MP_SMTP_AUTH} healthcheck: test: - CMD - /mailpit - readyz interval: 5s timeout: 20s retries: 10 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true
